title: Correlation Dashboard
subtitle: Multi-Pair Pearson Correlation Matrix (21 Pairs, Heatmap, Returns-Based)
author: Christian Benjamin (lynnchris)
verdict: EXTRACT — multi-pair correlation matrix architecture, returns-based correlation (better than price-based), AlertThreshold config, color-coded heatmap. Extends Part 20 from 1-pair to N-pair matrix.

core_concept.name: Correlation Matrix Dashboard
core_concept.description: Compute Pearson correlation between ALL unique pairs of a configurable instrument list. Default 7 symbols → 21 unique pairs. Uses simple returns (% change bar-to-bar) rather than raw closing prices for stationarity. Displays as color-coded heatmap table (green=positive, red=negative, intensity=strength).
core_concept.key_upgrade_vs_part_20: Part 20: 1 pair at a time. Part 22: full N×N matrix. Also switches from raw prices to returns — this is methodologically correct (prices are non-stationary; returns are).
core_concept.relevance_to_creviaderiv: Portfolio-level risk management. Before taking any new signal, compute correlation with all open positions using the returns-based method. Heatmap for monitoring dashboard.

key_insight.returns_vs_prices: Using returns (% change) rather than closing prices is statistically correct. Raw prices are non-stationary — correlation of prices can be spurious. Returns are stationary and correlation is meaningful.
key_insight.21_pairs_from_7: C(7,2) = 21 unique pairs. Scale: N symbols → N*(N-1)/2 pairs.
key_insight.alert_threshold: AlertThreshold=0.8 — when any pair hits this, alert the user. Maps to our position sizing gate.

keep: Full N×N matrix architecture — portfolio-wide view
LookbackBars=100 on H1 = ~4 trading days of correlation context
improve: Rolling window (50-bar) inside the 100-bar lookback to detect regime changes
Matrix output to dict for programmatic access (not just visual)
Correlation-adjusted position sizing: max_correlated_exposure = sum(positions weighted by |corr|)
